Name: adaptive_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Adaptive Mixed-Precision Quantization for Hardware-Optimized Inference
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic adaptation of mixed-precision quantization at the kernel level based on real-time profiling can significantly improve inference efficiency and accuracy on GPUs, addressing the limitations of existing static methods.
Related Work: Existing approaches, such as AMP-ViT and SNIP, focus on post-training quantization and adaptive mixed precision for model training. However, they do not leverage real-time profiling data from kernel-level execution to dynamically adjust quantization strategies, limiting their adaptability to hardware constraints. This proposal emphasizes the need for a hardware-centric approach that operates on actual execution traces, enabling more efficient precision allocation.
Abstract: As neural networks become increasingly complex, optimizing their inference performance through mixed-precision quantization is critical. Traditional methods often apply fixed precision across layers, leading to suboptimal performance and inefficient resource utilization. We propose an innovative adaptive mixed-precision quantization framework that dynamically adjusts bit-width allocations for different layers based on real-time profiling data collected during inference execution. By leveraging kernel-level metrics, such as memory bandwidth utilization and tensor-core occupancy, our approach aims to maximize throughput while minimizing latency. We will implement this framework on a commodity GPU, conducting extensive benchmarking to evaluate improvements in inference speed and accuracy. Our experiments will involve profiling various models and inferring under different workloads, enabling us to provide empirical evidence of performance gains. The proposed method not only enhances model efficiency but also offers insights into hardware-specific optimizations, setting the groundwork for future research in adaptive quantization techniques.
Experiments: Profile a set of models (e.g., ResNet, ViT, LLMs) to gather kernel-level performance metrics during inference.
Implement a dynamic quantization algorithm that adjusts layer precision based on the real-time profiling data.
Conduct A/B testing with fixed precision versus adaptive mixed precision across various batch sizes and input complexities.
Measure p50/p99 latencies, tokens-per-second throughput, and energy consumption under different configurations.
Determine the relationship between quantization levels and performance metrics using regression analysis to inform future adaptations.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Dependency on accurate profiling data; any inaccuracies may lead to suboptimal precision adjustments.
Potential overhead from real-time profiling might negate some of the performance gains achieved through quantization.
The approach may require extensive tuning to achieve maximum effectiveness across diverse model architectures.
